title: Indian Shuttlers Anmol, Rakshitha, Shriyanshi Advance at Macau Open
indian_angle: Three promising Indian women's singles players secured their spots in the next round of the Macau Open.
summary: Anmol Kharb, Rakshitha Ramraj, and Shriyanshi Valishetty all won their respective matches to move into the last 16 at the Macau Open. Anmol notably defeated Indonesia's Mutiara Ayu Puspitasari 21-13, 21-16.

title: BAI Issues Advisory Against Unauthorized Badminton Premier League Season 2
indian_angle: The national governing body, BAI, has warned players and officials about participating in an unsanctioned domestic league.
summary: Badminton Association of India has released an advisory regarding the 'Passion Sphere presents Badminton Premier League – Season 2,' stating it is an unauthorized event.

title: Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Tournament Announced for Chennai
indian_angle: This circular details a significant national ranking tournament crucial for Indian senior players to improve their standings.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has issued a circular for the Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament 2026, scheduled to take place in Chennai, Tamil Nadu from July 16th to 23rd, 2026.

title: Grassroot Coaches Development Program Circular for Guwahati & Raipur
indian_angle: This program is vital for nurturing coaching talent at the grassroots level in India, impacting future generations of players.
summary: BAI has released a circular for a Coaches Development Program at the Grassroot Level, to be held at NCE Guwahati & Raipur from July 15th to 24th, 2026.

title: Thomas Cup Squad Reassembles for Asian Games; Treesa-Gayatri Return
indian_angle: The core of India's historic Thomas Cup winning squad is regrouping, with the return of key doubles pair Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand, for the upcoming Asian Games.
summary: The Indian badminton squad, largely comprising players from the Thomas Cup winning team, is reassembling for the 20th Asian Games 2026, marking the return of the Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand women's doubles pair.

title: BAI Finalizes Team for 20th Asian Games 2026
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has officially finalized the national team that will represent India at the prestigious 20th Asian Games.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has announced the finalization of the team selected to represent India at the 20th Asian Games 2026.
